title: Programming Self-Assessment

elements.name: Introduction
elements.html:
Welcome!

We're excited that you are interested in taking an introductory programming course! Our CIS 2300 programming course is designed for all students, regardless of how much prior experience you do or do not have and regardless of your goals or reasons for taking the course. In that spirit, we want everyone to have the best experience in your first course, especially since prior experience can come in many different forms. It can be intimidating to be in a class full of students with prior experience and conversely being seasoned in a room of beginners. Since we offer multiple sections of 2300, this anonymous self-placement will suggest the most appropriate section for you.

elements.name: Background
elements.html:
If you have never programmed before, or if your programming experience is limited, you may choose in a cohort of similarly new students, designed for students with no previous experience whatsoever. If you took a high-school programming course intended to be similar to a first college course, like AP Computer Science A or IB Computer Science, we will suggest a section of CIS 2300 geared towards those with experience. This guided self-placement will help suggest the best section for you by asking you questions about your background. These will include questions about the specific courses and experience you have, how familiar you are with various topics and concepts, and how comfortable you are solving practice problems. We hope this will help most people have the right experience, but if you still have questions at the end, you can always contact us at informationsystemsandstatistics@baruch.cuny.edu for more support.

This self-placement is adaptive, meaning which questions you see and in which order will depend on your responses, so each student will have a slightly different experience, and if you go back and change your answers, you may see different questions appear. This is to help make sure we get the information that will most help us recommend the best course for you. As you go through the self-placement, you may also encounter terms or concepts you haven't heard of before. Don't be intimidated—if you haven't seen a topic or concept before, that just means you should probably be in the section that teaches it! We encourage you to answer all questions as truthfully as possible based on your current experience.

While CIS 2300 is taught in Python, you may have experience in other languages. Most concepts translate from one language to others, so be aware that some terms may be different.

When you're ready, click the button below to begin.

pages.name: code-conditionals
pages.title: Code Assessment: Conditionals
pages.description: Given the following code, answer the following questions:

elements.name: python_conditionals
elements.html:
x = 5
y = 10
z = 15
a = 20

if x > y and y < z:
	if z > a:
		print("z is the largest number.")
	else:
		print("a is the largest number.")
elif y > x and x < z:
	if z > y or True:
		print("z is the largest number.")
 	else:
 		print("y is the largest number.")
else:
 	if y > x:
 		print("y is the largest number.")
 	else:
 		print("x is the largest number.")

choices.text: a is the largest number
z is the largest number
y is the largest number

elements.name: python_conditionals_reaction
elements.title: Which of the following best describes your experience with the preceding problem?

choices.text: I did not understand the problem or did not know how to begin solving it.
I was able to begin solving the problem but got stuck.
I was able to complete the problem but struggled greatly.
I was able to solve the problem with a little difficulty.
I was able to solve the problem with no difficulty.

pages.name: code-for-loop
pages.title: Code Assessment: For Loops
pages.description: Given the following code, answer the following questions:

elements.name: python_for loop
elements.html:
def figure(max_num):
	for line in range(1, max_num + 1):
		for dots in range(1, max_num - line + 1):
			print(".", end="")
		print(line)

figure(8)

choices.text: Something else

I was able to solve the problem with a little difficulty.

pages.name: code-while-loop
pages.title: Code Assessment: While Loops
pages.description: Given the following code, answer the following questions:

elements.name: python_while loop
elements.html:
total_chars = 0
sentinel = "quit"
prompt = 'Type a word (or "' + sentinel +'" to quit): '
word = input(prompt)

while word != sentinel:
	total_chars += len(word)
	word = input(prompt)
	print("The total number of characters is:", total_chars)

choices.text: If the word entered is hi (then quit), the program prints The total number of characters is: 2
If the word entered is hi (then quit), the program prints The total number of characters is: 0
If the word entered is hi (then quit), the program prints The total number of characters is: 6

elements.title: Which of the following best describes your experience with the preceding problem?

choices.text: I did not understand the problem or did not know how to begin solving it.

I was able to complete the problem but struggled greatly.
I was able to solve the problem with a little difficulty.
I was able to solve the problem with no difficulty.

pages.name: code-dictionaries
pages.title: Code Assessment: Dictionaries
pages.description: Given the following code, answer the following questions:

elements.name: python_dictionaries
elements.html:
dict1 = {
	"a": {"apple", "avocado", "apricot"},
	"b": {"banana", "blueberry", "blackberry"},
	"c": {"cherry", "cranberry", "citrus"}
}

dict2 = {}

for key in dict1:
	for n in dict1[key]:
		dict2[n] = key

print(dict2)

choices.text: {'avocado': 'a', 'apple': 'a', 'apricot': 'a', 'banana': 'b', 'blueberry': 'b', 'blackberry': 'b', 'cherry': 'c', 'cranberry': 'c', 'citrus': 'c'}
{'a': 'apricot', 'b': 'blackberry', 'c': 'citrus'}
{'a': {'apple', 'avocado', 'apricot'}, 'b': {'banana', 'blueberry', 'blackberry'}, 'c': {'cherry', 'cranberry', 'citrus'}}

pages.name: code-return-values
pages.title: Code Assessment: Return Values
pages.description: Given the following code, answer the following questions:

elements.name: python_return values
elements.html:
def apply_discount(price, discount):
	saving = price * discount
	final = price - saving
	return final

result = apply_discount(80, 0.25)
print(result)

choices.text: 60.0
20.0
None

pages.name: code-advanced
pages.title: Code Assessment: Advanced Concepts
pages.description: Given the following code, answer the following questions:

elements.name: python_advanced
elements.html:
values = ["hello", "banana", "lime", "lemon", "apple",
	"strawberry", "grape", "kiwi"]
mystery1 = mystery(3, values)
print("Mystery 1:", mystery1)
mystery2 = mystery(5, values)
print("Mystery 2:", mystery2)

pairs = {}
for i in range(0, len(values), 2):
	pairs[values[i]] = values[i + 1]

print(pairs)

choices.text:
Mystery 1: False
Mystery 2: True
{'banana': 'hello', 'lemon': 'lime','strawberry': 'apple', 'kiwi': 'grape'}

Mystery 1: True
Mystery 2: False
{'hello': 'banana', 'lime': 'lemon', 'apple': 'strawberry', 'grape': 'kiwi'}

Mystery 1: False
Mystery 2: True
{'hello': 'banana', 'lime': 'lemon', 'apple': 'strawberry', 'grape': 'kiwi'}

pages.name: No Experience
pages.title: Placement Section A
pages.visibleIf: {#Previous AP IB Course} = false and {#preliminary_state} = 0 and {#concepts_state} = 0

elements.name: 2300 A1
elements.html:
CIS 2300, Section A, B, C, or D

Since you don't have any previous programming experience, we recommend you enroll in CIS 2300 section A, B, C, or D. This section will start from the very beginning and will help you learn basic programming skills along with other students with limited experience.

elements.name: 2300 A2
elements.html:
CIS 2300, Section A, B, C, or D

Since your programming experience is limited, we recommend you enroll in CIS 2300 section A, B, C, or D. This section will start from the very beginning and will help you learn basic programming skills along with other students with limited experience.

elements.name: 2300 A3
elements.html:
CIS 2300, Section A, B, C, or D

It sounds like you could use some refreshers on programming concepts. We recommend you enroll in CIS 2300 section A, B, C, or D. This section will start from the very beginning and will reinforce programming skills.

elements.name: 2300 B
elements.html:
CIS 2300, Section E, F, G, or H

Great! You seem to have some good prior knowledge. We suggest you enroll in section E, F, G, or H with other experienced students. This will ensure that the pace of instruction matches your skill level.

Thank you for completing the self-placement!

If you have any questions about your placement or which course is right for you, please contact us at informationsystemsandstatistics@baruch.cuny.edu

completedHtml:
Feel free to reach out with any questions!

Contact: informationsystemsandstatistics@baruch.cuny.edu

calculatedValues.name: preliminary_state
calculatedValues.expression: calculatePreliminaryState({Previous AP IB Course}, {AP CS A Score}, {IB Score}, {Previous Course}, {Previous Experience})
